# pystuff — LLM Reference

A personal Python toolkit for PyTorch development. Provides four public
symbols: PyStuff (unified entry point), Config, Logger, ShapeHook.
Not a production library — minimal, focused, and easy to extend.

Repository: https://github.com/jacksalici/pystuff
Package name: pystuff
Python: >= 3.11
Install: pip install -e . (from source, not on PyPI)

Torch is an OPTIONAL dependency. All classes use sys.modules.get('torch') at
call time rather than importing at module level. The package imports cleanly
without PyTorch. ShapeHook.register_hooks raises ImportError if torch is
absent. Config.get_device returns "cpu" silently if torch is absent.


## Module: pystuff — PyStuff (recommended entry point)

Unified initialiser that creates the Config singleton and a Logger singleton
together under a shared project name.

### Import

    from pystuff import PyStuff

### Constructor

    PyStuff(
        project_name: str,
        log_level: "debug" | "info" | "warning" | "error" = "info",
        report_to: "none" | "wb" | "tb" | "all" | None = None,
        remote_logger_run_name: str | None = None,
        tensorboard_log_dir: str = "./tb",
        log_separator: str = "|",
        log_multi_line: bool = True,
    )

### Attributes

    ps.config  -> Config   # the global Config singleton
    ps.logger  -> Logger   # the Logger singleton for this project

### Methods

    ps.parse(args=None) -> PyStuff
        Calls Config.instance().parse_cli_args(args). Returns self.
        Pass [] in tests to avoid reading sys.argv.

### Example

    from pystuff import PyStuff, Config

    @Config.configurable
    class Trainer:
        def __init__(self, lr=0.001, epochs=10):
            self.lr, self.epochs = lr, epochs

    ps = PyStuff("my_exp", report_to="tb")
    ps.parse()                    # or ps.parse(["--trainer.lr", "0.1"])
    ps.logger("Training started")
    trainer = Trainer()           # lr/epochs injected from CLI
    ps.logger({"loss": 0.42})
    ps.logger.close()


## Module: pystuff.shapehook — ShapeHook

Singleton. Attaches PyTorch forward hooks that print input/output tensor
shapes for every sub-module.

### Import

    from pystuff import ShapeHook

### Singleton access

    ShapeHook()               # get (or create) the singleton
    ShapeHook.instance()      # alternative; same as ShapeHook()
    ShapeHook.reset()         # destroy and recreate (use in tests)

### Methods

    register_hooks(model, one_time: bool = False) -> None
        Attach hooks to all named sub-modules of model.
        model must be a torch.nn.Module (checked at runtime).
        Raises ImportError if torch has not been imported yet.
        one_time=True: each hook removes itself after its first invocation.

    remove_model_hooks(model) -> None
        Remove all hooks attached to model.

    remove_all_models_hooks() -> None
        Remove all hooks across all registered models.

### Output format (printed to stdout)

    ShapeHook for <ClassName>   in shapes: [[...]]   out shape: [...]

### Example

    model = nn.Sequential(nn.Conv2d(3, 32, 3, padding=1), nn.ReLU())
    ShapeHook().register_hooks(model, one_time=True)
    model(torch.randn(1, 3, 32, 32))


## Module: pystuff.logger — Logger

Singleton class. Wraps Python logging with optional Weights & Biases and
TensorBoard integration. Log strings or metric dicts at severity levels.
Calling Logger() multiple times returns the same instance.

### Import

    from pystuff import Logger

### Singleton access

    Logger()                     # get (or create) the singleton
    Logger.instance()            # alternative; same as Logger()
    Logger.reset()               # destroy and recreate (use in tests)

### Constructor

    Logger(
        project_name: str,
        log_level: "debug" | "info" | "warning" | "error" = "info",
        report_to: "none" | "wb" | "tb" | "all" | None = None,
        remote_logger_run_name: str | None = None,
        tensorboard_log_dir: str = "./tb",
        separator: str = "|",
        multi_line: bool = True,
    )

    report_to values:
        "none" / None — console only
        "wb"          — console + Weights & Biases (requires wandb)
        "tb"          — console + TensorBoard (requires torch)
        "all"         — console + W&B + TensorBoard

### Calling

    logger(info: dict | str, level: str = "info", step: int | None = None)
        Log a string message or a dict of metrics.
        step: explicit TensorBoard step; otherwise uses internal counter.

### Other methods

    logger.print_config(config_dict: dict) -> None
        Log the config to console, W&B, and/or TensorBoard.

    logger.close() -> None
        Finish W&B run and close TensorBoard writer. Call at end of training.

### Example

    logger = Logger("my_project", log_level="info", report_to="tb")
    logger("Epoch started", level="info")
    logger({"loss": 0.42, "acc": 0.91})
    logger.close()


## Module: pystuff.config — Config

Singleton class. Central configuration manager combining:
- CLI argument parsing (argparse)
- YAML file loading
- Automatic __init__ injection via @Config.configurable decorator
Calling Config() multiple times returns the same instance.

### Import

    from pystuff import Config

### Singleton access

    Config()                  # get (or create) the global singleton
    Config.instance()         # alternative; same as Config()
    Config.reset()            # destroy and recreate (use in tests)

### Decorator: @Config.configurable

Registers all __init__ parameters (except self, *args, **kwargs) as
namespaced argparse flags at import time. At instantiation, missing arguments
are filled from the parsed config; explicit arguments always win.

    @Config.configurable
    class MyClass:
        def __init__(self, param=default_value, ...):
            ...
    # Registers: --myclassname.param

    @Config.configurable(prefix="custom")
    class MyClass:
        ...
    # Registers: --custom.param

    Rules:
    - Default prefix = cls.__name__.lower()
    - Duplicate prefixes raise ValueError at decoration time
    - *args and **kwargs are silently ignored
    - Parameters with no default are registered with default=None;
      if also missing from CLI, the original TypeError is raised at __init__

### Parsing (call once in entry point, after all imports)

    Config.instance().parse_cli_args()         # reads sys.argv
    Config.instance().parse_cli_args([...])    # explicit list (tests)

### Manual argument registration

    cfg.add_argument(
        name: str,
        default: Any = None,
        arg_type: type = str,
        help_text: str = "",
    )

    cfg.add_arguments_from_dict({
        "lr":     {"default": 0.001, "arg_type": float},
        "epochs": {"default": 10,    "arg_type": int},
    })

### YAML support

    cfg.load_from_yaml(path: str)              # merge one file
    cfg.load_from_multiple_yamls(paths: list)  # merge multiple files
    cfg.save_to_yaml(path: str)                # persist current config

    Load order: YAML → parse_cli_args. CLI values override YAML.

### Value access

    cfg.get(key: str, default=None) -> Any
    cfg.update_config(updates: dict)

### PyTorch helpers

    cfg.get_device() -> "cuda" | "mps" | "cpu"
        Reads config key "device" ("auto", "cuda", or "mps").
        Returns "cpu" silently if torch is not in sys.modules (no error).

    cfg.get_checkpoint_path() -> str
        Reads "checkpoint_path", "checkpoint_name", "model_name".
        Creates the directory if absent.

    cfg.ensure_directory(path: str)
        os.makedirs(path) if not exists.

### Full example

    # my_classes.py
    from pystuff import Config

    @Config.configurable
    class Trainer:
        def __init__(self, lr=0.001, epochs=10):
            self.lr = lr
            self.epochs = epochs

    @Config.configurable(prefix="opt")
    class Optimizer:
        def __init__(self, lr=0.01):
            self.lr = lr

    # main.py
    from my_classes import Trainer, Optimizer
    from pystuff import Config

    cfg = Config.instance()
    cfg.load_from_yaml("config.yaml")   # optional
    cfg.parse_cli_args()

    trainer   = Trainer()          # filled from CLI / YAML / defaults
    optimizer = Optimizer(lr=1e-3) # explicit arg overrides config

    # python main.py --trainer.lr 0.1 --trainer.epochs 50 --opt.lr 0.005


## File layout

    src/pystuff/
        __init__.py      # exports: PyStuff, Config, Logger, ShapeHook
        config.py        # Config class (singleton + decorator)
        logger.py        # Logger class
        shapehook.py     # ShapeHook class (singleton)
    tests/
        test_config.py   # unittest suite for Config and @Config.configurable
        test.py          # manual tests for Logger and ShapeHook
    pyproject.toml
    README.md
    llms.txt             # this file


## Key conventions

- PyStuff is the recommended entry point; it wires Config and Logger together
  under a shared project name with one constructor call.
- torch is optional everywhere. No top-level torch import anywhere in the
  package. sys.modules.get('torch') is used at call time.
- **Singleton pattern**: Config, Logger, and ShapeHook all use the same
  __new__() / __init__() pattern. Calling Config(), Logger(), or ShapeHook()
  multiple times returns the same instance. Each supports instance() and
  reset() class methods for consistent singleton access; reset() destroys
  and recreates (use in tests).
- Config.instance() and Config() are equivalent (both return the singleton).
  Logger.instance() and Logger() are equivalent (both return the singleton).
  ShapeHook.instance() and ShapeHook() are equivalent.
- @Config.configurable registers params at DECORATION time (import time).
  Call ps.parse() / parse_cli_args() AFTER all decorated classes are imported
  and BEFORE instantiating them if you rely on CLI injection.
- CLI flags use dot notation: --<prefix>.<param>. The dot is part of the key.
- CLI-injected values are plain strings. Use add_argument(arg_type=...) or
  add_arguments_from_dict for coercion. @Config.configurable does NOT coerce.
- wandb and TensorBoard are optional. Logger degrades gracefully if absent.
